title: Forward your mail with USPS
description: Set up mail forwarding at usps.com to catch anything still going to your old address. It lasts 12 months, but you should still update your address with all senders directly.

title: Update your driver's license
description: Most states require you to get a new license within 30–90 days of establishing residency. Check your new state's DMV requirements — some require an in-person visit and a new photo.

title: Register your vehicle in the new state
description: Vehicle registration requirements vary by state. You may need a new inspection, emissions test, or title transfer. Deadlines are usually 30–60 days after establishing residency.

title: Register to vote at your new address
description: Update your voter registration to your new address and state. Check deadlines — some states require registration 30 days before an election. Many states offer online registration.

title: Update your address with banks and credit cards
description: Update your mailing address with every financial institution — banks, credit cards, investment accounts, and lenders. This keeps statements and tax documents flowing to the right place.

title: Update address with insurance providers
description: Notify your auto, health, renter's/homeowner's, and life insurance providers. Your rates and coverage may change based on your new location.

title: Find a new primary care doctor
description: Search your insurance provider's directory for in-network physicians near your new address. Schedule an initial visit so you have an established relationship before an urgent need arises.

title: Find a new dentist
description: Don't let this slip — dental issues get worse with neglect. Find an in-network dentist and schedule a cleaning, especially if it's been a while since your last visit.

title: Transfer prescriptions to a local pharmacy
description: Call your new pharmacy and provide your old pharmacy's details — they'll handle the transfer. Alternatively, ask your doctor to send new prescriptions to the new location.

title: Request copies of your medical records
description: Ask your previous doctors to send your medical records to your new providers. This ensures continuity of care, especially for ongoing conditions or specialists.

title: Find a nearby hospital and urgent care
description: Locate the nearest emergency room and urgent care facility, and save their addresses in your phone. Know which ones are in-network for your insurance.

title: Set up utilities at your new home
description: Arrange electricity, gas, water, sewer, trash, internet, and any other utilities. Schedule start dates to align with your move-in day so everything is on when you arrive.

title: Hire movers or rent a truck
description: Get quotes from multiple moving companies, check reviews, and confirm insurance coverage. If doing it yourself, reserve a truck early — especially around month-end when demand spikes.

title: Declutter and donate before packing
description: Moving is the perfect time to downsize. Donate, sell, or toss anything you won't use. Every box you don't pack saves time, money, and effort on both ends.

title: Get a storage unit if needed
description: If there's a gap between your move-out and move-in dates, or if your new place is smaller, rent a storage unit. Climate-controlled units protect sensitive items.

title: Explore your new neighborhood
description: Walk or drive around to find the nearest grocery store, coffee shop, pharmacy, post office, gas station, and parks. Getting oriented quickly makes the new place feel like home.

title: Find local community groups and meetups
description: Search for local groups on Meetup, Facebook, Nextdoor, or through community centers. Joining a running club, book club, or hobby group is one of the fastest ways to build a social circle.

title: Find a gym or fitness option
description: Research gyms, yoga studios, climbing gyms, or recreational leagues near your home or work. Many offer free trial periods, so test a few before committing.

title: Locate pet services if you have pets
description: Find a new veterinarian, groomer, and dog walker or pet sitter. Transfer your pet's medical records and update their microchip registration with your new address.

title: Understand your new state's tax situation
description: Income tax rates, property tax rules, and sales tax vary dramatically by state. Understand how the move impacts your tax obligations, especially if you moved mid-year.

title: Update your employer's records
description: Notify HR of your new address for tax withholding purposes. If you've moved to a different state, your state income tax withholding will change.

title: Update auto insurance for the new location
description: Car insurance rates vary significantly by ZIP code. Notify your insurer of the move — your premium will be recalculated based on your new location's risk profile.

title: Research public transit options
description: Look into buses, trains, bike-share programs, and ride-share availability in your new city. A transit pass may save you money and reduce the headache of parking.

title: Update toll transponders and parking permits
description: If you use an E-ZPass, SunPass, or similar toll transponder, update your address and payment info. Cancel old city parking permits and apply for new ones if needed.

title: Set up identity monitoring
description: A move increases your exposure to identity theft — new accounts, forwarded mail, and change-of-address filings are all vulnerability points. Consider an identity monitoring service during the transition.
